data.description:
## Context / Problem
Plan-review findings can tell a client to comply with rebar-internal identifiers such as "ADR 0043." Those ADRs live in the rebar source repository, are not part of a client project's architecture record, and are not meaningful or necessarily accessible to the plan author. The defect is currently grounded in bundled reviewer instructions including src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E2.md, plan_review_E6.md, plan_review_F1.md, plan_review_T13.md, plan_review_T14.md, and plan_review_hedge.md, plus deterministic operator-attested coaching in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_operator_attested.py and src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py.

## Approach
Replace rebar-internal ADR citations in reviewer-facing prompt instructions and deterministic finding/coaching templates with self-contained plain-language rules. Preserve the underlying behavior, such as requiring the exact [operator-attested] tag and concrete ticket evidence, but explain it without assuming knowledge of rebar's design history.

Add a portability regression that runs plan review against a fixture client repository with no rebar docs/adr tree and asserts that reviewer-authored finding, impact, suggested-fix, remediation, and coaching text contains no rebar-internal ADR identifier. Add a source-level contract test covering bundled plan-review prompt text and deterministic user-facing message templates so future internal provenance references cannot leak into client output. Internal Python comments, rebar's own ADR documents, and client-authored/cited ADRs that exist in the target repository remain allowed.

Rejected: shipping or linking rebar's ADR corpus to every client. That would expose implementation history instead of making findings self-contained and would still couple client guidance to rebar internals.

## Scope
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E2.md
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E6.md
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_F1.md
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T13.md
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T14.md
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_hedge.md
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_operator_attested.py
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py
- tests/unit/test_plan_review_portability.py

## Testing
- Deterministic path: an untagged operational-evidence AC still produces actionable coaching, but no rebar ADR identifier.
- LLM prompt path: the fixture client has no rebar ADR files; generated finding fields state the applicable rule in plain language.
- Source contract: bundled plan-review prompt bodies and deterministic client-facing templates reject rebar-internal ADR tokens while permitting internal code comments and an ADR citation grounded in a client fixture.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An operator-attested plan-review fixture produces the same criterion decision before and after the wording change; verified by pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_portability.py.
- [ ] Reviewer-authored finding, impact, suggested_fix, remediation, and coaching fields for a client fixture with no rebar ADR files contain no rebar-internal ADR identifier; verified by pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_portability.py.
- [ ] Bundled plan-review prompt bodies and deterministic user-facing message templates contain no rebar-internal ADR citation; verified by pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_portability.py.
- [ ] A client-authored ADR citation backed by a file in the target repository remains eligible as grounded evidence; verified by pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_portability.py.
- [ ] Internal implementation comments and docs/adr files remain outside the portability restriction; verified by the source-contract test's explicit scan scope.
- [ ] The default non-integration suite exits zero; verified by pytest -m "not integration and not external" -q.
data.title: Plan-review findings leak rebar-internal ADR references
